name: SIZZLING RICE crepes
intro: These crepes are yellow and folded over like an omelet, but don’t contain eggs; they’re crisp like the bottom of a paella, but no rice grains are visible. Bánh xèo rice crepes are in a class of their own. The southern Viet charmers are named for the sizzling sound they make while cooking and typically contain pork, shrimp, mushroom, and bean sprouts. Snipped or broken into pieces and eaten as lettuce-and-herb wraps with nước chấm dipping sauce, the crepes hit all pleasure centers.

ingredients.item: white rice flour (such as Bob’s Red Mill)
tepid water mixed with ¾ cup freshly boiled water (rest boiled water 1 minute and then measure), plus more water as needed
full-fat unsweetened coconut milk (shake or stir before using)
ground pork (85% lean), chicken thigh, or beef chuck, roughly chopped to loosen
medium white mushrooms or fresh shiitake mushrooms, thinly sliced, stems included
small red or yellow onion, thinly sliced
Canola or other neutral oil for cooking
Leaves from 1 large head soft-leaf lettuce (such as butter, Boston, or red or green leaf)
small handful mint, basil, or other soft-leaf fresh herbs (except cilantro)

method: To make the batter In a medium bowl, whisk together the rice flour, cornstarch, salt, and turmeric. Whisk in the water and then the coconut milk. Let the yellow batter sit, uncovered, for 30 to 45 minutes, to thicken to the consistency of half-and-half (it will be slightly gritty from the flour). Whisk in more water, 1 teaspoon at a time, to thin out the batter, if needed (when I have to add water, it’s usually no more than 1 tablespoon). The batter may be made up to 2 days ahead and stored, covered, in the refrigerator; bring to room temperature before using.

To prep the filling In order to cook efficiently with less mess, pre-portion the filling components. Divide the pork, shrimp, mushrooms, and onion onto six small pieces of parchment paper, creating a pile of goodies for each crepe. Sprinkle each portion with a pinch of salt. Set on a tray or baking sheet and keep near the stove with the batter and bean sprouts.
